title: Class-4 Impact-Resistant Shingles in Texas: Is the Insurance Discount Worth It?
angle: Walk through the actual math on Class-4 IR shingle in NW Houston — what the upgrade costs, which TX carriers give premium discounts and at what rate, and how many years it takes to pay back. The answer is nearly always 'yes, get the upgrade' — but the post shows the work.
mustInclude: What UL 2218 Class 4 means (tested with 2-inch steel ball impact, no evidence of fracture/damage)
Price delta vs standard architectural: ~$1,800-$3,200 on a typical Woodlands home
Discount range by carrier: Allstate 5-30%, State Farm 10-27%, Farmers up to 25%, USAA up to 30%, Progressive up to 25%, Nationwide up to 25% — wind/hail portion only
Realistic payback math: typical 3-6 year breakeven
Specific brands: IKO Nordic, Malarkey Legacy, GAF Grand Sequoia IR, CertainTeed NorthGate
How to file the premium-discount form with your carrier (we help)
Honest downside: aesthetic differences vs standard architectural (some lines have fewer color options)
mustNotInclude: no claim that any shingle is 'hail-proof' (no shingle is; Class 4 is impact-resistant, which is different)
no specific carrier discount percentages presented as guaranteed (vary by individual policy)
